title: 10 possible side effects of Capsicum annuum (Cayenne) if used improperly
intro: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell peppers or chili peppers, can have adverse health effects if used improperly. When consumed in excess, they can cause stomach irritation, nausea, and diarrhea due to their capsaicin content. Prolonged exposure to the skin or mucous membranes of capsaicin can result in irritation, pain, and inflammation. In rare cases, allergic reactions to Capsicum annuum can occur, leading to symptoms such as hives, swelling, and difficulty breathing. Moreover, improper handling and consumption of peppers contaminated with harmful bacteria such as Salmonella can result in severe foodborne illnesses. Therefore, it is crucial to handle and consume Capsicum annuum properly and in moderation to avoid potential health risks.
